title: pelargonium micranthum
intro: Pelargonium micranthum, also known as the small-flowered pelargonium or Cape geranium, is a shrubby perennial plant native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its essential oil composition. The health benefits of Pelargonium micranthum include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce fever and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Pelargonium micranthum are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
